channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Consultant Opportunity: Generation Unlimited – UNICEF South Africa
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
UNICEF South Africa is hiring a consultant to support its Generation Unlimited initiative, which aims to connect 1.5 million young people to economic opportunities by 2030. This is an exciting opportunity for professionals passionate about youth empowerment, innovation, and impact-driven work. About the Role The selected consultant will support efforts to expand access to economic […]

The post Consultant Opportunity: Generation Unlimited – UNICEF South Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
UNICEF South Africa is hiring a consultant to support its Generation Unlimited initiative, which aims to connect 1.5 million young people to economic opportunities by 2030.

This is an exciting opportunity for professionals passionate about youth empowerment, innovation, and impact-driven work.

About the Role

The selected consultant will support efforts to expand access to economic opportunities for young people across South Africa.

This includes contributing to:

Program strategy and implementation
Innovative solutions for youth employment
Partnership development and stakeholder engagement
Monitoring and impact-driven initiatives

Who Should Apply

This role is ideal for individuals who:

Have a strong passion for youth development
Bring innovative thinking and problem-solving skills
Have experience in program management, partnerships, or social impact work
Are interested in working within an international development context

Why This Opportunity Matters

The Generation Unlimited initiative is a global effort to ensure that young people have access to education, employment, and entrepreneurship opportunities.

By joining this work, you contribute directly to shaping the future of youth livelihoods and economic inclusion.

How to Apply

Apply here.

For more Job Opportunities , Visit OFY here.

The post Consultant Opportunity: Generation Unlimited – UNICEF South Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Rise on Boards Program – Applications Open
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Rise on Boards Program by Fora Network for Change, a leadership initiative designed to equip young women and gender-marginalized youth with the skills and confidence to serve on boards and lead effectively. This global program is open to both national and international applicants, making it accessible no matter where […]

The post Rise on Boards Program – Applications Open appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Rise on Boards Program by Fora Network for Change, a leadership initiative designed to equip young women and gender-marginalized youth with the skills and confidence to serve on boards and lead effectively.

This global program is open to both national and international applicants, making it accessible no matter where you are in the world.

About the Program

The Rise on Boards stream provides a structured learning experience focused on governance, leadership, and decision-making.

Participants will engage in:

Live training sessions
Applied, hands-on learning
Mentorship from experienced leaders

What You’ll Gain

Knowledge of governance and board readiness
Confidence navigating power and accountability
Strategic decision-making skills
Access to a global network of peers and mentors

Who Should Apply

Young women and gender-marginalized youth aged 18–29
Individuals passionate about community engagement and leadership
Applicants interested in training delivered in English or French

Key Details

Deadline: 30 April 2026 (11:59 PM EST)
Eligibility: Open globally
Program Type: Leadership / Training Program

Why This Opportunity Matters

The program is designed to close leadership gaps by empowering underrepresented voices to take up space in governance and decision-making roles across sectors.

How to Apply

Learn more and apply here

For more Training opportunities , Visit OFY here.

The post Rise on Boards Program – Applications Open appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Documentary Lab Fellowship for Filmmakers of Color
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Documentary Lab, a 12-month fellowship designed to support emerging filmmakers of color working on their first or second feature documentary. This program focuses on helping storytellers refine and complete their projects while building the professional skills needed to succeed in the documentary film industry. About the Fellowship The Documentary […]

The post Documentary Lab Fellowship for Filmmakers of Color appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Documentary Lab, a 12-month fellowship designed to support emerging filmmakers of color working on their first or second feature documentary.

This program focuses on helping storytellers refine and complete their projects while building the professional skills needed to succeed in the documentary film industry.

About the Fellowship

The Documentary Lab provides a comprehensive support system that includes:

Professional development opportunities
Customized mentorship from industry experts
A project grant of $25,000 to support your documentary

For the 2026 cycle, the program will only accept projects currently in post-production, making it ideal for filmmakers nearing completion.

Who Should Apply

This fellowship is best suited for:

Filmmakers of color
Creators working on their first or second feature documentary
Projects that are already in post-production
Storytellers looking to strengthen both their film and industry network

Program Benefits

Receive one-on-one mentorship tailored to your project
Gain access to industry insights and professional guidance
Secure funding to help complete your documentary
Join a network of emerging filmmakers and creative professionals

Key Details

Duration: 12 months
Grant: $25,000 project funding
Deadline: 4 May 2026

Why This Opportunity Matters

The Documentary Lab is designed to amplify underrepresented voices in film by providing the resources, funding, and mentorship needed to bring important stories to life.

How to Apply

Learn more and apply here.

For more Fellowship opportunities, Visit OFY here.

The post Documentary Lab Fellowship for Filmmakers of Color appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: PhD Opportunity: Arts & Children’s Social Development
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
A unique, fully funded PhD is now open at the University of Amsterdam (UvA), exploring how performing arts shape young children’s social development. This interdisciplinary opportunity combines psychology, developmental science, and the performing arts, offering a rare chance to study creativity and human development together. Why This PhD is Unique Study the impact of live […]

The post PhD Opportunity: Arts & Children’s Social Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
A unique, fully funded PhD is now open at the University of Amsterdam (UvA), exploring how performing arts shape young children’s social development.

This interdisciplinary opportunity combines psychology, developmental science, and the performing arts, offering a rare chance to study creativity and human development together.

Why This PhD is Unique

Study the impact of live performing arts on children
Combine social psychology and developmental science
Use behavioural, physiological, and computational methods
Collaborate with festivals, artists, and cultural institutions

What You’ll Do

Design and conduct lab and real-world experiments with children
Collect and analyze behavioural and physiological data
Explore concepts such as empathy, synchrony, and imagination
Work closely with families, artists, and researchers
Publish findings and present at international conferences

Who Should Apply

Candidates with a Master’s degree in Psychology or a related field
Strong quantitative and coding skills
Experience or interest in working with children
Strong communication and organizational skills
Interest in interdisciplinary research

Key Details

Location: Amsterdam, Netherlands
Start Date: September 2026
Application Deadline: 13 May 2026
Salary: €3,059 – €3,881 per month (fully funded)

What You’ll Gain

Work within the Amsterdam Arts & Social Sciences Lab
Access to advanced research labs and facilities
Join a strong international research network
Benefit from training, conferences, and career development opportunities

Why This Matters

This PhD explores how art shapes empathy, connection, and imagination in children, contributing to improved education, wellbeing, and social development.

How to Apply

Apply here

For more PHD opportunities, Visit OFY here.

The post PhD Opportunity: Arts & Children’s Social Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded African Research Fellowships 2026 by the European Space Agency (ESA): For African Scientists to Work in Italy
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The African Research Fellowships Call 2026 under the European Space Agency is one of the most competitive and prestigious research opportunities currently open to African scholars. Designed to empower the next generation of scientists, this fully funded fellowship provides African researchers with the chance to work at the ESA’s ESRIN research center in Italy, collaborate […]

The post Fully Funded African Research Fellowships 2026 by the European Space Agency (ESA): For African Scientists to Work in Italy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The African Research Fellowships Call 2026 under the European Space Agency is one of the most competitive and prestigious research opportunities currently open to African scholars. Designed to empower the next generation of scientists, this fully funded fellowship provides African researchers with the chance to work at the ESA’s ESRIN research center in Italy, collaborate with global experts, and develop cutting-edge solutions using satellite data and advanced technologies.

If you are an African researcher seeking international exposure, access to world-class facilities, and the opportunity to contribute to solving Africa’s most pressing challenges, this fellowship could be a life-changing opportunity.

About the African Research Fellowship Programme

The African Research Fellowship (ARF) is part of the EO AFRICA initiative, a flagship program aimed at strengthening collaboration between Africa and Europe in Earth Observation (EO) science and innovation.

Since its launch in 2021, the program has supported several African researchers, enabling them to conduct impactful research and contribute to solving environmental, agricultural, and socio-economic challenges across the continent.

Selected fellows will spend one full year at ESA’s ESRIN center, working closely with leading scientists and accessing advanced research infrastructure.

Who Can Apply? (Eligible Candidates)

This fellowship is specifically designed for:

Eligible Category of People

  • African researchers and scientists
  • Early-career to mid-level researchers
  • Individuals passionate about Earth Observation and satellite data

Eligible Countries/Regions

  • All African countries are eligible

This means applicants from:

  • East Africa (Kenya, Uganda, Tanzania, etc.)
  • West Africa (Nigeria, Ghana, Senegal, etc.)
  • Southern Africa (South Africa, Zambia, Zimbabwe, etc.)
  • North Africa (Egypt, Morocco, Tunisia, etc.)

are all encouraged to apply.

Academic Qualifications and Requirements

To qualify for this fellowship, applicants must meet the following criteria:

Minimum Academic Requirements

  • A PhD degree, OR
  • A Master’s degree with substantial research experience

Relevant Fields of Study

Applicants should have a background in:

  • Earth Observation (EO)
  • Remote sensing
  • Satellite data science
  • Environmental science
  • Geospatial analysis
  • Engineering or related disciplines

Additional Requirements

  • Strong research experience and publication record
  • Ability to develop a high-quality research proposal
  • Passion for solving African challenges using technology

Research Focus Areas

Applicants are required to submit a 1-year research proposal aligned with EO AFRICA priorities. Key areas include:

  • Climate and environmental monitoring
  • Water resource management
  • Food security and agriculture
  • Land use and ecosystem analysis
  • Urban development and sustainability

The research must leverage:

  • Satellite Earth Observation data
  • Cloud computing platforms
  • Artificial Intelligence (AI) and advanced modelling techniques

Fellowship Benefits

This is a fully funded fellowship, offering a wide range of benefits:

Financial Benefits

  • Monthly subsistence allowance
  • Travel expenses covered
  • Relocation support package

Academic & Professional Benefits

  • Work at ESA’s ESRIN research center in Italy
  • Access to high-performance computing tools
  • Collaboration with top European scientists
  • Participation in EO AFRICA Space Academy

Career Advancement

  • Exposure to international research networks
  • Opportunity to publish high-impact research
  • Enhanced career prospects in academia and industry

What Fellows Will Do

Selected fellows will:

  • Conduct innovative research using satellite data
  • Develop solutions for real-world African challenges
  • Collaborate with European and African scientists
  • Contribute to open science and data-sharing initiatives
  • Build tools and insights that inform policy and development

The program emphasizes translating scientific data into practical, scalable solutions for Africa.

Required Application Documents

Applicants must submit a complete application package, including:

  1. Research Proposal (max 10 pages using official template)
  2. Curriculum Vitae (max 3 pages)
  3. Valid Identification (passport preferred)
  4. Academic Certificates (PhD or Master’s)
  5. Two Signed Reference Letters

Failure to follow the required format may result in disqualification.

Apply here

Why You Should Apply

This fellowship is more than just funding—it is a gateway to becoming a leader in Earth Observation science in Africa.

Key reasons to apply:

  • Gain international research experience in Europe
  • Work with cutting-edge satellite and AI technologies
  • Contribute to solving Africa’s biggest challenges
  • Join a prestigious network of global scientists
  • Enhance your academic and professional profile

Deadline

29th April 2026

The African Research Fellowships Call 2026 represents a rare and highly valuable opportunity for African researchers to step onto the global stage. With full funding, access to world-class facilities, and a strong focus on innovation and impact, this fellowship is ideal for ambitious scientists ready to make a difference.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Fully Funded African Research Fellowships 2026 by the European Space Agency (ESA): For African Scientists to Work in Italy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Frank Jennings Fellowship April 2026: Fully Funded Human Rights Fellowship in Ireland and Geneva for Global Graduates
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
If you are passionate about human rights advocacy, international law, and global justice, the Frank Jennings Fellowship April 2026 offers one of the most prestigious early-career opportunities in the world. Hosted by Front Line Defenders in partnership with the Office of the United Nations High Commissioner for Human Rights (OHCHR), this fellowship provides hands-on experience […]

The post Frank Jennings Fellowship April 2026: Fully Funded Human Rights Fellowship in Ireland and Geneva for Global Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are passionate about human rights advocacy, international law, and global justice, the Frank Jennings Fellowship April 2026 offers one of the most prestigious early-career opportunities in the world. Hosted by Front Line Defenders in partnership with the Office of the United Nations High Commissioner for Human Rights (OHCHR), this fellowship provides hands-on experience working directly with human rights defenders at risk across the globe.

What is the Frank Jennings Fellowship?

The Frank Jennings Fellowship is a highly competitive international fellowship designed to train and mentor the next generation of human rights professionals. It was established to honor Frank Jennings, a key figure in advancing human rights activism.

The program provides a unique combination of NGO and UN-level experience, allowing fellows to work both with an international organization and within the United Nations system.

Participants gain deep exposure to:

  • Protection of human rights defenders
  • International advocacy systems
  • UN human rights mechanisms
  • Policy drafting and global reporting processes

Fellowship Structure (Detailed Breakdown)

The fellowship is divided into three intensive phases, each designed to build expertise progressively:

Phase 1: Dublin, Ireland – Training with Front Line Defenders (3–3.5 Months)

During this phase, fellows receive foundational training and practical exposure to human rights protection work.

Key responsibilities include:

  • Supporting protection coordinators
  • Drafting urgent appeals for at-risk defenders
  • Managing databases and case documentation
  • Conducting research on global human rights issues

Phase 2: Geneva, Switzerland – Internship with OHCHR (6 Months)

This is the core international experience where fellows work directly within the UN system.

Key responsibilities include:

  • Monitoring global human rights violations
  • Drafting communications to governments
  • Contributing to Human Rights Council reports
  • Supporting UN Special Rapporteur mandates

Phase 3: Return to Dublin (3 Months)

Fellows return to apply their newly acquired UN experience in a practical NGO environment.

Activities include:

  • Supporting ongoing human rights cases
  • Contributing to advocacy projects
  • Strengthening field-based protection mechanisms

Financial Benefits

The fellowship is fully funded with a monthly stipend, making it accessible to candidates worldwide.

  • Dublin: Approximately €1,651/month
  • Geneva: Approximately €1,700/month

This financial support helps cover living expenses during the program.

Eligibility Criteria

This fellowship is specifically designed for early-career professionals and recent graduates with a strong commitment to human rights.

Who Can Apply?

  • Recent university graduates (within one year of graduation)
  • Final-year students eligible to graduate before the fellowship begins
  • Individuals with demonstrated interest in human rights work

Academic & Professional Requirements

Applicants must have:

  • Knowledge of international human rights law and the UN system
  • Strong research, writing, and analytical skills
  • Administrative and organizational skills
  • Ability to work in multicultural environments

Language Requirements

  • Fluency in English (mandatory)
  • Fluency in Spanish (preferred) or French (often required)

Additional Requirements

  • Legal authorization to work in Ireland
  • Strong communication and teamwork abilities
  • Previous experience in human rights organizations (advantageous but not always mandatory

Eligible Countries and Regions

The fellowship is open to applicants globally, meaning candidates from:

  • Africa
  • Europe
  • Asia
  • North America
  • South America
  • Oceania

can apply, provided they meet the eligibility criteria.

However, applicants must ensure they meet visa/work authorization requirements, especially for Ireland.

Key Skills and Competencies Required

To stand out, applicants should demonstrate:

  • Strong policy writing and briefing skills
  • Ability to synthesize complex information
  • Research and analytical thinking
  • Understanding of global human rights frameworks
  • Cultural awareness and adaptability

Application Requirements

Applicants must typically submit:

  1. CV/Resume
  2. Cover Letter (explaining motivation and experience)
  3. Proof of language proficiency
  4. Supporting documents (if required)

Make sure your application highlights:

  • Your commitment to human rights
  • Relevant academic or field experience
  • Your interest in international advocacy

Apply here

Why You Should Apply

The Frank Jennings Fellowship is not just a training program—it is a career-launching opportunity that offers:

  • Direct experience with the United Nations
  • Hands-on work with global human rights cases
  • Professional networking with international experts
  • A pathway into careers in NGOs, UN agencies, and international law

This fellowship is particularly valuable for those aiming to work in:

  • International organizations
  • Policy and advocacy
  • Human rights law
  • Global development

Deadline

14th April 2026

The Frank Jennings Fellowship April 2026 stands out as one of the most impactful fellowships for aspiring human rights professionals worldwide. With its combination of NGO and UN experience, financial support, and global exposure, it provides an unparalleled platform to build a meaningful career in international human rights.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Frank Jennings Fellowship April 2026: Fully Funded Human Rights Fellowship in Ireland and Geneva for Global Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Youth Innovating Democracy 2026 in Strasbourg, France: Fully Funded Council of Europe Youth Event on Shaping the Future of Democracy
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The Youth Innovating Democracy 2026 event, organized by the Council of Europe in partnership with the European Youth Forum, is a prestigious, fully funded international youth event designed to empower young leaders to actively contribute to the future of democracy in Europe and beyond. Held at the European Youth Centre in Strasbourg, France from June […]

The post Youth Innovating Democracy 2026 in Strasbourg, France: Fully Funded Council of Europe Youth Event on Shaping the Future of Democracy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Youth Innovating Democracy 2026 event, organized by the Council of Europe in partnership with the European Youth Forum, is a prestigious, fully funded international youth event designed to empower young leaders to actively contribute to the future of democracy in Europe and beyond.

Held at the European Youth Centre in Strasbourg, France from June 24 to June 26, 2026, this unique event is part of the consultation phase of the New Democratic Pact for Europe, an ambitious initiative aimed at reinforcing democratic systems, promoting inclusivity, and addressing emerging challenges such as disinformation, political disengagement, and democratic backsliding.

The programme will bring together up to 120 young participants from across Europe and beyond to engage in meaningful dialogue, develop innovative solutions, and directly influence policy recommendations that will shape the future of democracy.

About the Youth Innovating Democracy Event

The Youth Innovating Democracy event is a flagship youth engagement initiative under the New Democratic Pact for Europe. It is designed as a platform for young people to:

  • Share their perspectives on democracy and governance
  • Propose innovative solutions to strengthen democratic systems
  • Engage directly with policymakers and political leaders
  • Contribute to shaping future democratic frameworks

This event reflects the Council of Europe’s long-standing commitment to youth participation in democratic decision-making processes.

Why This Event Matters

Across Europe and globally, democracy faces increasing challenges, including:

  • Democratic backsliding
  • Disinformation and misinformation
  • Declining trust in institutions
  • Youth underrepresentation in politics

The New Democratic Pact for Europe aims to address these issues by strengthening democratic foundations, amplifying citizen participation, and innovating governance systems.

The Youth Event plays a critical role in this process by ensuring that young people are at the center of these conversations and solutions.

Key Themes of the Event

The event is structured around three major thematic areas that address pressing challenges in democracy.

1. Juvenate Politics: Innovating the System

This theme focuses on increasing youth participation in political systems.

Key discussions include:

  • Youth representation in decision-making
  • Electoral reforms (e.g., youth quotas, voting age)
  • Civic education and political engagement
  • Inclusion of young people in governance

2. Live Equality: The Promise of Democracy

This theme addresses social and economic inequalities affecting young people.

Key focus areas:

  • Access to social rights
  • Economic justice and inclusion
  • Impact of crises on youth (economic, political, health)
  • Addressing marginalization and exclusion

3. Empower Communities: Building Participation Ecosystems

This theme explores how to create enabling environments for youth participation.

Topics include:

  • Role of youth organizations and civil society
  • Participatory democracy models
  • Community engagement and grassroots initiatives
  • Protection of democratic freedoms

Objectives of the Programme

The event aims to:

  • Engage youth in shaping the New Democratic Pact for Europe
  • Highlight innovative practices in youth participation
  • Foster dialogue between youth and policymakers
  • Promote democratic values and human rights
  • Build a network of young leaders

Programme Structure

The three-day programme includes:

  • Plenary sessions with political leaders
  • Interactive workshops and discussions
  • Creative and participatory activities
  • Networking opportunities
  • Development of policy proposals and outputs

Participants will contribute directly to recommendations and creative outputs that will inform the New Democratic Pact.

Participant Profile

The event seeks a diverse group of young people, including:

  • Representatives of international youth organizations
  • Members of national or local youth councils
  • Youth activists in democracy and human rights
  • Participants from youth movements and initiatives
  • Youth delegates and policy contributors
  • Young artists, journalists, and content creators

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Aged between 18 and 35 years
  • Resident in a country party to the European Cultural Convention (exceptions possible)
  • Able to work in English or French
  • Available for the full duration of the event
  • Able to contribute to follow-up actions after the event

Selection Process

  • Up to 120 participants will be selected
  • Selection ensures diversity in:
    • Gender
    • Geographic representation
    • Experience levels
    • Cultural backgrounds

A waiting list will also be established.

Financial Support and Benefits

The event is fully funded by the Council of Europe.

Covered Costs

  • Travel expenses (according to Council of Europe rules)
  • Accommodation (European Youth Centre or nearby hotel)
  • Meals during the event

Participants are expected to arrive on June 23 and depart on June 27, 2026.

Working Languages

  • English
  • French

Participants must be able to communicate effectively in at least one of these languages.

Learn more HERE.

Why You Should Apply

This event offers a unique opportunity to:

  • Engage directly with European policymakers
  • Influence the future of democracy
  • Build international networks
  • Develop leadership and advocacy skills
  • Gain global exposure

Application Process

Applicants must:

  • Complete the Official Online Application Form
  • Submit before the deadline

Applications must be submitted through the official platform; incomplete or late applications will not be considered.

Application Deadline

  • Deadline: April 19, 2026 (23:59 CET)

Please read the call for applications for more information.

Discover more global Conference opportunities on OFY.

The post Youth Innovating Democracy 2026 in Strasbourg, France: Fully Funded Council of Europe Youth Event on Shaping the Future of Democracy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: West Africa Democracy Fund 2026 by TrustAfrica: Grants up to $500,000 for Civil Society, Youth, and Governance Innovation Projects
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The West Africa Democracy Fund (WADF) 2026, launched by TrustAfrica, is a transformative funding opportunity aimed at strengthening democratic governance, civic participation, and institutional accountability across West Africa. This major grant initiative supports organizations working at local, national, and regional levels to reimagine governance systems and amplify citizens’ voices in shaping their political and social […]

The post West Africa Democracy Fund 2026 by TrustAfrica: Grants up to $500,000 for Civil Society, Youth, and Governance Innovation Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The West Africa Democracy Fund (WADF) 2026, launched by TrustAfrica, is a transformative funding opportunity aimed at strengthening democratic governance, civic participation, and institutional accountability across West Africa. This major grant initiative supports organizations working at local, national, and regional levels to reimagine governance systems and amplify citizens’ voices in shaping their political and social futures.

Backed by leading global philanthropic organizations—including the Ford Foundation, MacArthur Foundation, Luminate Group, Open Society Foundations, and Carnegie Corporation of New York—the WADF represents a significant investment in advancing democracy, inclusion, and resilience in the region.

With grants ranging from $50,000 to $500,000, this programme offers a powerful opportunity for civil society organizations, youth groups, and institutions to implement impactful, innovative, and scalable governance solutions.

About the West Africa Democracy Fund (WADF)

The West Africa Democracy Fund was officially launched in Abuja, Nigeria, as a response to the evolving democratic challenges across the region. The fund seeks to support initiatives that:

  • Strengthen democratic institutions
  • Promote inclusive governance
  • Enhance citizen engagement
  • Foster accountability and transparency

At its core, WADF is about rethinking and redefining the role of citizens in governance systems across West Africa.

Programme Vision and Purpose

The WADF aims to:

  • Expand democratic and civic spaces
  • Strengthen social contracts between governments and citizens
  • Promote inclusive political participation
  • Support resilient and accountable governance systems

The fund emphasizes the importance of citizen voices in shaping governance outcomes and future political systems.

Key Objectives of the Fund

The WADF focuses on several strategic objectives that guide funded projects:

  1. Incorporating Citizens’ Perspectives
    • Integrate citizen aspirations into public policy and development agendas
  2. Strengthening Social Contracts
    • Promote frameworks that guide elections, media use, and governance systems
  3. Mobilizing Communities
    • Engage youth, religious groups, media, trade unions, and civil society
  4. Fostering Regional Collaboration
    • Encourage partnerships across countries and institutions
  5. Strengthening Public Institutions
    • Support institutions like the judiciary, electoral bodies, and media regulators

Thematic Areas

Applicants must align their proposals with one or more of the following thematic areas:

1. Multi-Stakeholder Dialogues

Projects may include:

  • Public consultations and town hall meetings
  • Community dialogue platforms
  • Cultural and religious engagement spaces
  • Policy discussions and social cohesion initiatives

Expected outcomes:

  • Policy recommendations
  • Community-driven governance solutions
  • Strengthened citizen-government relationships

2. Advocacy for Institutional Reform

Eligible activities include:

  • Advocacy for policy and governance reforms
  • Strengthening participation of marginalized groups
  • Engagement with regional institutions such as ECOWAS
  • Promoting democratic frameworks and governance protocols

3. Strengthening Strategic Public Institutions

Projects may focus on:

  • Judicial systems
  • Electoral management bodies
  • Media and telecommunications regulators

Support may include:

  • Capacity building
  • Technical assistance
  • Institutional reform strategies

4. Innovative Projects

The fund encourages creative and forward-thinking ideas that:

  • Reimagine democracy
  • Introduce new governance models
  • Address emerging challenges in the region

Grant Categories

Applicants can apply under different funding levels:

  • Small Grants: Minimum $50,000
  • Medium Grants: Up to $250,000
  • Large Grants: Up to $500,000

Project Duration

  • 12 to 24 months

Application Formats

  • Single organization
  • Consortium
  • Network-based applications

Eligibility Criteria

Organizations must meet the following requirements:

Geographic Eligibility

Applicants must be:

  • Based in or operating within:
    • Burkina Faso
    • Ghana
    • Mali
    • Niger
    • Nigeria
    • Senegal
    • Togo

Regional organizations working across these countries are also eligible.

Organizational Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Be legally registered organizations
  • Have experience in:
    • Governance
    • Democracy
    • Citizen engagement
    • Political participation
  • Demonstrate:
    • Financial management capacity
    • Experience managing similar grant sizes

Consortium Applications

  • Lead organization must have prior experience managing similar projects or regranting

What the Fund Does Not Support

The WADF does not fund:

  • General institutional support (non-project-based funding)
  • Election monitoring activities (except specific use cases)
  • Partisan political activities
  • Lobbying for specific legislation
  • Organizations on restricted sanction lists

Why You Should Apply

The West Africa Democracy Fund offers:

  • Significant funding for impactful projects
  • Opportunity to influence governance systems
  • Support for innovative and scalable solutions
  • Regional and global recognition
  • Collaboration opportunities across West Africa

Application Process

Applicants must complete the application through the Official Platform.

Required Documents

  • Completed application form
  • Organizational and project budgets
  • Legal registration certificate
  • Audited financial statements (if applicable)
  • CVs of key staff (maximum of five)

Submission Guidelines

  • Applications must be submitted via the Fluxx platform
  • Submissions can be in English or French
  • Email or paper applications will not be accepted

Application Timeline

  • Application Deadline: April 30, 2026

Applicants are encouraged to submit early to avoid last-minute challenges.

Additional Opportunities

Applicants can also:

  • Participate in an information webinar (April 13, 2026)
  • Contact the project manager for support

Click HERE to learn more about the West Africa Democracy Fund (WADF) 2026.

Visit Opportunities for youth for more global Grant opportunities.

The post West Africa Democracy Fund 2026 by TrustAfrica: Grants up to $500,000 for Civil Society, Youth, and Governance Innovation Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Digital Civics Academy 2026: Empowering the Next Generation of Ethical Tech Leaders
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
The rapid rise of artificial intelligence and digital platforms has created unprecedented opportunities—and challenges—for young people around the world. The Digital Civics Academy (DCA) 2026 is stepping up to equip the next generation with the tools, confidence, and leadership skills needed to shape a healthier digital future. This transformative one-week summer program is designed specifically […]

The post Digital Civics Academy 2026: Empowering the Next Generation of Ethical Tech Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The rapid rise of artificial intelligence and digital platforms has created unprecedented opportunities—and challenges—for young people around the world. The Digital Civics Academy (DCA) 2026 is stepping up to equip the next generation with the tools, confidence, and leadership skills needed to shape a healthier digital future.

This transformative one-week summer program is designed specifically for teens who are passionate about advocacy, technology, and creating meaningful change in their communities.

What Is the Digital Civics Academy?

The Digital Civics Academy is an immersive, youth-centered masterclass co-designed by young people, for young people. It focuses on empowering participants to become ethical leaders in technology and digital wellbeing.

The 2026 theme, “Agency in the Age of Artificial Intelligence,” explores how youth can responsibly navigate emerging technologies while protecting their mental health and digital wellbeing.

Participants will gain hands-on experience in activism, storytelling, and leadership—key skills needed to influence both local and global conversations around technology.

Program Dates and Format

  • Program Duration: July 20–24, 2026
  • Format: Primarily virtual
  • Optional In-Person Opportunities:
    • New York City
    • San Francisco
    • Washington, DC
    • Los Angeles

The hybrid model ensures accessibility while still offering unique real-world exposure for those who can attend in person.

Application Deadlines

  • Early Decision Deadline: April 15, 2026 (11:59 PM PDT)
    • Decision released by the end of April
  • Regular Decision Deadline: April 30, 2026 (11:59 PM PDT)
    • Decision released mid to late May

Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply early due to limited capacity.

What You Will Learn

Participants of the Digital Civics Academy will develop both technical and interpersonal skills essential for leadership in the digital age.

Core Learning Areas:

  • Advocacy and civic engagement
  • Multimedia storytelling
  • Leadership and collaboration
  • Policy and legislation translation
  • Digital wellbeing and self-advocacy

Students will also engage directly with policymakers, tech designers, and industry leaders, gaining insights into how decisions are made and how they can influence them.

Real-World Exposure and Opportunities

Past cohorts have had the opportunity to:

  • Visit major tech companies like Pinterest and Snapchat
  • Speak with legislators at the California State Capitol
  • Learn from influential leaders, including policymakers and media professionals

These experiences provide participants with a rare behind-the-scenes look at how technology, policy, and advocacy intersect.

Mentorship and Expert Guidance

The program is facilitated by trained team members and industry experts who lead workshops and masterclasses. Participants will learn directly from professionals shaping the future of AI, policy, and digital wellbeing.

Cost and Accessibility

One of the most compelling aspects of the Digital Civics Academy is that it is completely free of charge. Thanks to generous sponsorships, students from diverse backgrounds can participate without financial barriers.

Who Should Apply?

The program is ideal for young individuals who:

  • Are passionate about technology and social impact
  • Want to develop leadership and advocacy skills
  • Are eager to collaborate with like-minded peers
  • Have a desire to make meaningful change

No prior experience in advocacy or technology is required, making this an inclusive opportunity for beginners and experienced changemakers alike.

Why You Should Apply

The Digital Civics Academy is more than just a summer program—it is a launchpad for young leaders who want to shape the future of technology responsibly.

With access to expert mentorship, real-world experiences, and a global network of peers, participants leave the program equipped to drive meaningful change in their communities and beyond.

If you are ready to become a voice for ethical technology and digital wellbeing, this is your opportunity to step forward.

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more summer programs opportunities, click here. 

The post Digital Civics Academy 2026: Empowering the Next Generation of Ethical Tech Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Cameron Impact Scholarship 2026 (Class of 2027): Fully Funded Undergraduate Scholarship in the USA
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
The Cameron Impact Scholarship 2026 is a highly competitive, fully funded undergraduate scholarship designed for exceptional high school students who demonstrate leadership, academic excellence, and a deep commitment to creating positive change in their communities and the world. Each year, approximately 15 students are selected to receive this prestigious four-year scholarship, which supports their academic […]

The post Cameron Impact Scholarship 2026 (Class of 2027): Fully Funded Undergraduate Scholarship in the USA appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Cameron Impact Scholarship 2026 is a highly competitive, fully funded undergraduate scholarship designed for exceptional high school students who demonstrate leadership, academic excellence, and a deep commitment to creating positive change in their communities and the world.

Each year, approximately 15 students are selected to receive this prestigious four-year scholarship, which supports their academic journey at any accredited U.S. college or university.

What the Cameron Impact Scholarship Covers

This scholarship provides comprehensive financial support, including:

  • Full tuition
  • All mandatory fees
  • Books and academic materials

Recipients have the flexibility to attend any accredited four-year college or university in the United States, making it one of the most versatile fully funded scholarships available.

Who Should Apply?

The Cameron Impact Scholarship is looking for students who go beyond academic success. Ideal applicants are:

  • Visionary leaders with a strong sense of purpose
  • Actively involved in community service or civic engagement
  • Passionate about making a measurable impact
  • Committed to long-term contributions toward the greater good

This program values impact-driven individuals who aspire to become future change-makers.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Minimum GPA of 3.7 (unweighted) or equivalent
  • Must be a U.S. citizen
  • Member of the high school graduating Class of 2027
  • Planning to enroll full-time in a four-year U.S. college or university
  • Demonstrated excellence in leadership, extracurricular activities, and community service

Application Requirements

To apply, candidates must submit:

  • Completed online application
  • Two letters of recommendation (one from school personnel)
  • Academic transcript (unofficial copies accepted)

Incomplete applications will not be reviewed, and no extensions are granted.

Step-by-Step Application Process

To successfully apply for the Cameron Impact Scholarship, follow these steps carefully:

  1. Fill out the Applicant Profile form on the official website
  2. Create a username and password
  3. Activate your account via email (check spam folder if needed)
  4. Log in through the “Applicant Login” portal
  5. Complete the application and essays (pay attention to word limits)
  6. Upload your transcript and two recommendation letters
    • Alternatively, documents can be emailed to: admin@bryancameronef.org
  7. Submit your application

Important Tips:

  • The system does not allow saving and returning later, so prepare your answers in advance (e.g., in Word)
  • Disable browser cookies to avoid technical errors
  • Ensure all required documents are submitted—incomplete applications will not be considered

Selection Process

The selection process includes:

  • Rolling application review
  • Selection of approximately 100 finalists
  • In-person interviews in Fall 2026
  • Final selection and announcement in December 2026

Recipients are also required to attend an annual awards ceremony during the scholarship period.

Equity in a Merit-Based Program

The Cameron Impact Scholarship evaluates applicants through an equity-focused lens, considering:

  • Individual circumstances and available opportunities
  • Challenges faced and overcome
  • Impact achieved relative to resources

This ensures that excellence is recognized across diverse backgrounds and experiences.

Application Deadline

May 1, 2026, at 12:00 PM PT (Noon)

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis and will close once 3,000 completed and eligible applications are received or by the deadline—whichever comes first. Early submission is strongly recommended.

Why You Should Apply

This scholarship is more than financial support—it is an investment in future leaders. Benefits include:

  • Full financial coverage for your undergraduate degree
  • Access to a powerful network of changemakers
  • Opportunities for collaboration and global impact

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more scholarship opportunities, click here. 

Final Thoughts

The Cameron Impact Scholarship 2026 is a life-changing opportunity for high school students who are ready to lead, serve, and create meaningful change. With limited slots and a rolling application process, applying early can significantly improve your chances.

Prepare thoroughly, submit early, and take a bold step toward becoming a global leader.

The post Cameron Impact Scholarship 2026 (Class of 2027): Fully Funded Undergraduate Scholarship in the USA appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
